claim: Explorer evidence is factually sound: all code paths, test assertions, and artifact data exist and match the claimed JSON repair implementation and stress test observations. One minor line citation error exists but evidence remains valid.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate() verified with four repair regex patterns: line ranges (42-88 to quoted), backslash escaping, empty key names, trailing commas
evidence.why: Core repair logic exists as claimed at cited lines

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject() verified with three extraction strategies: direct parse, fenced code block, sliced object extraction
json_repaired field verified: records parsedOutput.repaired === true in provider_runs
normalizeAgentResult() verified: returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null
evidence.why: Fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker code exists as claimed

evidence.summary: Test verified: injects drift patterns (42-88, .yolo backslash, empty key) and asserts json_repaired === true
evidence.why: Repair test validates exact drift patterns claimed

evidence.summary: Test verified: 'not json' output returns status=blocked with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON in agent_results and blockers
evidence.why: Fail-closed test validates explicit blocker code as claimed

evidence.summary: provider_runs verified: json_repaired: false for explorer and verifier roles, parse_error: empty
evidence.why: Stress test data confirms no real drift observed yet (explorer cited wrong lines 624-660 but data exists at 380-417)

evidence.summary: Problem statement verified: 'Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch must handle minor Claude JSON key drift and fail closed with explicit blockers'
evidence.why: Evidence this exact demand was previously run in stress tests

risks: If real Claude agents produce drift outside the four implemented regex patterns, JSON will be rejected as EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON even though potentially recoverable
Repair patterns are theoretical: stress tests show json_repaired: false across all real agent executions, so patterns have not been validated against actual Claude drift

result.notes: Evidence verification complete. All source code, tests, and artifacts exist and support the claim. One minor issue: explorer cited stress test lines 624-660 but file has only 604 lines; actual provider_runs data is at lines 380-417. Evidence remains valid despite citation error. Claim stands: JSON repair logic exists with 4 specific regex patterns, fail-closed behavior is implemented, tests validate both modes, but stress tests show real Claude agents have not triggered repair yet (all show json_repaired: false). Risks are valid and appropriately identified.
